perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will not use any Confidential Information, except as agreed upon between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Purpose' as a specific use case. The modified text removes this definition but continues to reference the handling of confidential information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the scope of permitted use of Confidential Information is now undefined, creating uncertainty around the term 'Purpose' referenced in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "Confidential Information" ​ means information that relates to the Purpose (as defined below) or that, although not related to such Purpose, is nevertheless disclosed as a result of the parties' discussions in that regard, and that should reasonably have been understood by the party receiving such information (the “Recipient”), because of legends or other markings, the circumstances of disclosure or the nature of the information itself, to be proprietary and confidential to the party disclosing the information (the “Discloser”) or an Affiliate of the Discloser or to a third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the specific 'Purpose' for using Confidential Information. The changed text removes this definition, making the permitted use vague ('except as agreed upon between the parties'). The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'the Purpose (as defined below)', but now there's no clear definition of Purpose, creating a direct contradiction and ambiguity in interpreting the scope of 'Confidential Information'.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose and who are legally bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to Recipient under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information to its employees as deemed necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits disclosure to those with a “need to know” and who are bound by similar confidentiality agreements. The modified text removes both restrictions. This creates an in-text contradiction because the 'need to know' condition is removed, potentially conflicting with the reasonable care standard outlined in Section 2 (Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care.).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, except as such disclosure is expressly permitted in this Agreement. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits disclosure of confidential information to those with a 'need to know' and who are bound by similar confidentiality agreements. The changed text removes both restrictions, allowing disclosure to any employee as deemed necessary. The contradicted text requires Recipient to treat Discloser’s Confidential Information with 'reasonable care'. By removing the 'need to know' restriction, the changed text permits potentially broad disclosure that might not align with the 'reasonable care' standard. This creates a contradiction because the Recipient could disclose information to employees who don't need it, arguably failing to treat the information with 'reasonable care', causing a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees not to copy or reverse engineer, or attempt to derive the composition or underlying information, structure or ideas of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees not to reverse engineer any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits copying, reverse engineering, and attempts to derive underlying information. The modified text only prohibits reverse engineering. This creates an in-text contradiction because other sections still imply the protection of all forms of Confidential Information, but only reverse engineering is explicitly prohibited, weakening the overall protection of Discloser's confidential information. For example section 8 states that the “Confidential Information is unique and valuable”. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient.
perturbation.justification: The original text protected Confidential Information from copying, reverse engineering, and attempts to derive underlying information. The changed text only prohibits reverse engineering. The Perturbation Explanation states that this creates an in-text contradiction because section 8 implies protection of all forms of Confidential Information, but only reverse engineering is explicitly prohibited, weakening protection. The Contradicted Text states that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and any breach will cause irreparable damage. By removing the explicit prohibitions on copying and deriving underlying information, the modified text weakens the overall protection, thus contradicting the statement that any breach will cause irreparable damage. The change directly conflicts with the notion that 'Confidential Information is unique and valuable and a breach...will cause irreparable and continuing damage' by reducing the scope of prohibited actions, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement will be and will remain the property of the Discloser; all such information in tangible form will be returned to Discloser promptly upon written request or upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and will not thereafter be retained in any form by Recipient or its Affiliates or any of their employees.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement will be and will remain the property of the Discloser; all such information in tangible form will be returned to Discloser promptly upon written request.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return of confidential information upon request or termination and prohibits its retention. The modified text removes the prohibition on retention after termination. This creates an in-text contradiction if the contract elsewhere assumes that all confidential information is returned and not retained after termination, affecting the obligations outlined in Section 6, which states that obligations under section 2 survive the termination. Removing the mandate to return and 'not retain in any form' weakens this obligation in section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will become effective as of the Effective Date and will continue until the date one party receives written notice of termination of this Agreement from the other party; provided, however, that a Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will survive termination of this Agreement and will continue with respect to the Discloser’s Confidential Information until the obligations no longer apply pursuant to Section 3 above.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the retention of confidential information after termination, supporting the survival clause in Section 6. The changed text removes this prohibition, directly contradicting the intent of Section 6, which requires obligations related to confidential information to survive termination. By allowing retention, the changed text weakens the enforceability of the recipient's obligations regarding confidentiality after the agreement ends, creating a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a breach or threatened breach of the obligations under this Agreement, Discloser will be entitled to: (1) seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting a bond, and (ii) indemnification from any loss or harm, including recovery of attorney’s fees, in connection with any breach or enforcement of Recipient’s obligations hereunder or the unauthorized use or release of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a breach or threatened breach of the obligations under this Agreement, Discloser will be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: The original text outlines that the Discloser is entitled to seek (1) specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting a bond, and (ii) indemnification from any loss or harm, including recovery of attorney’s fees. The modified text removes the right to indemnification and recovery of attorney's fees in the case of a breach of contract. Removing the part where the Discloser is entitled to an indemnification and recovery of attorney’s fees, affects the protection to confidential information in Section 1 and creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "Confidential Information" ​ means information that relates to the Purpose (as defined below) or that, although not related to such Purpose, is nevertheless disclosed as a result of the parties' discussions in that regard, and that should reasonably have been understood by the party receiving such information (the “Recipient”), because of legends or other markings, the circumstances of disclosure or the nature of the information itself, to be proprietary and confidential to the party disclosing the information (the “Discloser”) or an Affiliate of the Discloser or to a third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Discloser to seek indemnification and attorney's fees in the event of a breach. The changed text removes this right. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information.' By removing the right to indemnification and recovery of attorney's fees in the case of a breach, the modified text weakens the protection of confidential information. The Discloser no longer has the right to indemnification and recovery of attorney's fees. This creates an in-text contradiction because the incentive to protect this information is significantly reduced, thus breaking compatibility between the definition of confidential information and the changed remedies for breaches of obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties irrevocably consents to the exclusive personal jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in New Castle County, Delaware as applicable, for any matter arising out of or relating to this Agreement, except that in actions seeking to enforce any order or any judgment of the federal or state courts located in New Castle County, Delaware such personal jurisdiction will be non-exclusive. Additionally, notwithstanding anything in the foregoing to the contrary, a claim for equitable relief arising out of or related to this Agreement may be brought in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties consents to the personal jurisdiction of courts as applicable for any matter arising out of or relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies exclusive jurisdiction in New Castle County, Delaware, with an exception for enforcement actions and allowing equitable relief claims to be brought elsewhere. The modified text removes the specification of jurisdiction in Delaware and the exceptions. This creates an in-text contradiction with the governing law clause, Section 10(c), which states that the agreement will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware. If the agreement is governed by Delaware law, but jurisdiction isn't clearly established in Delaware, it could lead to disputes about where legal actions should be brought.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement (a) is the complete agreement of the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements whether oral or written with respect to disclosures concerning such subject matter; (b) may not be amended or modified except by in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties; and (c) will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware without regard to conflict of laws provisions.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies exclusive jurisdiction in New Castle County, Delaware. The changed text removes this specification, which directly contradicts Section 10(c) of the contradicted text, which states that the agreement will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware. Removing the Delaware jurisdiction creates an in-text contradiction because the governing law is Delaware, but the changed text allows legal actions to potentially be brought elsewhere, leading to uncertainty and conflict.
